---
title: "A smooth environment keeps the class moving"
---

## Outcome

Advanced users leave setup mode quickly and get to runnable Python with minimal friction.

:::: {.lesson-grid}
::: {.lesson-panel}
### VS Code

Use one editor for Quarto, Python, terminal work, and Copilot support.
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
### `uv`

Create environments and run projects with short, repeatable commands.
:::

::: {.lesson-panel}
### Copilot

Use it as a coding assistant, not as a replacement for understanding the build.
:::
::::

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A(Install tools) --> B(Create project)
    B --> C(Sync dependencies)
    C --> D(Run sample server)
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Baseline commands

Use `uv lock`, `uv sync`, and `uv run` as the repeated rhythm for the afternoon.
:::
